description_long: This house features a traditional half-timbered facade with exposed wooden beams and infill panels, reminiscent of medieval European architecture. The roof is steeply pitched and covered with dark shingles, and there are multiple chimneys visible along the roofline. The front elevation displays a combination of tall, narrow windows and a prominent entryway at street level, while the side and rear elevations show a mix of timber-framed and plain plastered walls. The windows are vertically oriented and vary in size, with some grouped together and others spaced apart. The structure appears to be two stories tall, with the upper story slightly overhanging the lower in some sections. The overall appearance suggests a historic or period-style urban residence, likely found in older European city centers.
description: A two-story, half-timbered house with a steep shingle roof, tall narrow windows, and a prominent entryway, evoking historic European urban architecture.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway, tall windows, and detailed half-timbered facade.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the mix of exposed timber framing and plain plastered wall, with two chimneys visible on the roof.
description_view_2: Rear or alternate side view with more visible timber framing, a small door, and fewer windows.
description_view_3: Back view dominated by a large expanse of half-timbered wall and plaster, with minimal window openings and two chimneys.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with vertically oriented windows
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway at street level with a simple door, no porch, and framed by timber elements.
